skill.name: firecrawl-agent
skill.description: Use Firecrawl Agent to extract structured data from complex websites. It can navigate pages, follow user-provided goals, and return schema-guided results.
skill.summary: Extract structured website data with Firecrawl Agent.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static findings for markdown backticks, relative documentation links, and weak cryptography are false positives. The real risk is legitimate but elevated: the skill grants wildcard Firecrawl CLI execution, performs network extraction, and supports file output paths.

medium_findings.title: Wildcard Firecrawl CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The allowed-tools block permits Bash(firecrawl *) and Bash(npx firecrawl *). This is needed for the skill, but wildcard command arguments increase impact if prompts or parameters are abused.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash allowlist is explicit and uses wildcard arguments. The intent appears legitimate because all examples use the Firecrawl CLI for the documented extraction workflow.

medium_findings.title: External Network Extraction By Design
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to navigate websites and run Firecrawl Agent commands. User goals, URLs, schemas, and extracted page data may be processed through an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented purpose is autonomous website extraction. This is not malicious, but it creates clear privacy and compliance considerations for target URLs and extracted data.

low_findings.title: Output And Schema File Path Handling
low_findings.description: The documented CLI options allow schema files and output paths. These are normal Firecrawl features, but users should avoid sensitive paths and review generated files before sharing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path options are visible in examples and the options table. No evidence shows forced overwrite, unauthorized reads, or malicious path construction.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick findings point to markdown code fences, inline option formatting, and tips. They do not show Ruby execution or shell substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown syntax or inline code labels. They are documentation text, not executable Ruby or shell backtick expressions.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings are relative markdown links to neighboring Firecrawl skill documents. They are not file access logic and do not traverse user files at runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation links under the See also section. No runtime filesystem operation or user-controlled path resolution is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic functions, hashes, ciphers, or password handling. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are frontmatter description text and a markdown table header. There is no cryptographic implementation in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Bash Tool Permission
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands with arbitrary trailing arguments. This should be treated as an external command execution permission.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration directly shows wildcard Bash permissions for Firecrawl commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: CLI Output Path Argument
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents output file path support through -o and --output. This can write generated extraction results to local paths chosen in the command.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples and option table show output path usage, but no malicious path is specified.

content.user_title: Extract Structured Website Data with Firecrawl
content.value_statement: Complex websites often require navigation before useful data appears. This skill uses Firecrawl Agent to gather structured results from pages, URLs, and schema-guided extraction tasks.
actual_capabilities: Runs Firecrawl Agent commands for autonomous website extraction tasks.
Uses starting URLs to focus extraction on specific pages.
Accepts inline JSON schemas for predictable structured results.
Supports schema files for reusable extraction definitions.
Writes extraction results to local output files when requested.
Supports credit limits for longer agent runs.
limitations: Requires Firecrawl CLI or npx access in the active environment.
Requires valid Firecrawl credentials and available credits.
Agent runs can take several minutes on complex websites.
Results depend on website access, page stability, and extraction instructions.

use_cases.title: Compare SaaS Pricing Pages
use_cases.description: Extract pricing tiers, plan names, limits, and add-ons from several product pages.

use_cases.title: Build Product Research Tables
use_cases.description: Collect product names, prices, specifications, and availability from category pages.

use_cases.title: Create Directory Datasets
use_cases.description: Pull organization names, contacts, locations, and profile links from public directories.

prompt_templates.title: Extract One Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner extraction from a known page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl Agent to extract the key data from this page: [URL]. Save the results under .firecrawl/.

prompt_templates.title: Use A Simple Schema
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured extraction with fields defined by the user.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract product names, prices, and short descriptions from [URL]. Use a schema so each item has consistent fields.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Across Multiple Pages
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate research across a site section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Start from [URL] and extract all pricing tiers, feature limits, billing terms, and source page links.

prompt_templates.title: Constrain Cost And Output
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced extraction with budget and file controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Firecrawl Agent on [URL] with a credit limit. Use my schema file and save the reviewed output in .firecrawl/results.json.

output_examples.input: Extract pricing tiers from a SaaS pricing page.
output_examples.output: A structured summary with plan names, monthly prices, included limits, highlighted features, and source page references.

output_examples.input: Collect product listings from a category page.
output_examples.output: A table-ready dataset containing product names, prices, descriptions, availability status, and product page links.

output_examples.input: Extract entries from a public directory.
output_examples.output: A clean list of organizations with contact details, locations, categories, and profile links where available.

best_practices: Provide a narrow goal, target URLs, and expected fields before starting the agent.
Use schemas for repeatable output when downstream tools need stable columns.
Set credit limits and save outputs under a project-local .firecrawl directory.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad extraction tasks without a clear scope or credit limit.
Do not send private, regulated, or confidential data unless Firecrawl use is approved.
Do not write outputs to sensitive or shared paths without reviewing the destination.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It runs Firecrawl Agent to navigate websites and return structured data for extraction tasks.

faq.question: Does it require a Firecrawl account?
faq.answer: Yes. The environment needs Firecrawl CLI access, credentials, and enough credits for the requested run.

faq.question: Can it use a schema?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports inline schemas and schema files to make extracted fields more predictable.

faq.question: Where are results saved?
faq.answer: The examples save results under .firecrawl, but users can choose other output paths.

faq.question: Is this better than a simple scrape?
faq.answer: Use it for complex, multi-page extraction. Use simpler scraping for one stable page.

faq.question: What security review is needed?
faq.answer: Review URLs, schemas, output paths, and data sensitivity before running network extraction commands.
